summary: PRC Rate Consistency Investigation: Backend API is WORKING CORRECTLY. All 11 tests pass. The /api/admin/prc-rate/current endpoint returns consistent rate (11) across all calls. No user-specific rate fields exist in user documents. The issue is likely PRODUCTION-SPECIFIC (different database) or BROWSER CACHING (PWA service worker).
action_items: PRODUCTION INVESTIGATION NEEDED: This is a production issue - preview database shows consistent rate (11)
Check production database economy factors - they may calculate to 12 instead of 11
Ask affected users to clear browser cache and PWA data
Consider adding cache-busting headers to /api/admin/prc-rate/current endpoint
Add timestamp to rate response so frontend can detect stale cached values
critical_code_review_comments: Backend API /api/admin/prc-rate/current is correctly implemented - returns global rate from dynamic economy calculation
Frontend DashboardModern.js correctly fetches rate from API at lines 159, 163, 198
Service worker correctly excludes /admin/ paths from caching (line 78-82 of service-worker.js)
No user-specific prc_rate field exists in user documents
Rate is calculated using 5 economy factors and cached for 5 minutes (ECONOMY_CACHE_TTL = 300)
The raw_rate in preview is 10.58 which rounds to 11 - production may have different factors resulting in 11.5+ which rounds to 12
context_for_next_testing_agent: PRC rate API is working correctly in preview. Issue is production-specific. Backend returns consistent rate 11 across all API calls. No user-specific rate override exists. Possible causes: 1) Production database has different economy factors, 2) Browser/PWA caching old rate values.

investigation_findings.issue_reported: Different users see different PRC rates (11 vs 12)
investigation_findings.user_document_check: No prc_rate field found - rate is truly global
possible_root_causes: PRODUCTION DATABASE: Different economy factors may calculate to 12 instead of 11
BROWSER CACHING: Users may have cached old rate from when it was 12
PWA CACHING: Service worker may have cached old API response before exclusion was added
RATE CHANGE: If rate recently changed from 12 to 11, some users see old cached value
recommended_actions: Check production /api/admin/prc-rate/current to see actual rate
Check production /api/prc-economy/current-rate to see economy factors
Ask affected users to: 1) Clear browser cache, 2) Uninstall and reinstall PWA, 3) Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R)
Consider adding no-cache headers to rate endpoint

tests_executed.test_prc_rate_api_returns_consistent_rat: PASS - 10 consecutive calls returned same rate (11)
tests_executed.test_dashboard_endpoint_has_no_user_spec: PASS - Dashboard doesn't return user rate
tests_executed.test_redeem_categories_has_no_user_speci: PASS - No rate in redeem categories
tests_executed.test_economy_factors_are_valid: PASS - All factors within expected ranges
tests_executed.test_multiple_users_see_same_rate: PASS - Simulated users get same rate
tests_executed.test_rate_api_is_not_user_authenticated: PASS - API is global, no auth required
